behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is command-to-artifact-store wiring (key/slot routing), not a single pure function. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of a serializer would miss wrong slot writes between modules. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required by behavior text; in-process store is enough, so not System. LLM-DEP: behavior is about artifact destination, not model output quality, so not Agentic. MULTI-STEP: single stage only, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only requires 'stores grounding into a dedicated grounding artifact, not the plan artifact' (local routing contract).
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command with an in-memory artifact repository preloaded with a `plan` artifact. Assert command writes expected payload to `grounding` and leaves `plan` unchanged.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at interaction boundary: implement flow must read one artifact slot and write another while preserving purpose. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only fallback logic or parser would miss cross-module handoff errors. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, so not System. LLM-DEP: correctness here is routing/usage of grounding and output destination, not whether model content is semantically good, so not Agentic. MULTI-STEP: this behavior is one stage’s IO contract, not full pipeline, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected higher levels because text is specifically 'reads grounding when available and still writes ... to plan' (deterministic local contract).
behaviors.test_description: Execute implement-stage flow against an in-memory artifact store containing `grounding`. Assert grounding is consumed (or referenced by the flow) and resulting execution artifact is written to `plan`, with `grounding` preserved.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Bug is side-effect control across deep-dive stage + issue-content reader + artifact writer modules. MOCK-MISS: a unit test on 'diagnosis exists' predicate alone would not catch unintended write calls. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text; can model issue content and artifacts in-process, so not System. LLM-DEP: behavior checks absence of redundant write, not quality of generated diagnosis, so not Agentic. MULTI-STEP: single stage side effect, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected higher levels because behavior text focuses on 'no longer overwrites plan ... when diagnosis already exists in issue content' (local orchestration/state guard).
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content with existing diagnosis and run deep-dive stage with an instrumented in-memory artifact repository. Assert no write to `plan` occurs and pre-existing artifacts remain unchanged.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets full pipeline correctness across multiple stages and temporal state propagation. MULTI-STEP: yes, by text ('full multi-step agent run'). LLM-DEP: end-stage reflection often depends on agent-produced intermediate content and synthesis, so real agentic chaining risk exists. Lower-level tests can validate individual contracts but can miss chain-level loss/override of grounding. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration/Agentic because behavior text requires end-to-end sequence `deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`, i.e., pipeline-level guarantee, not single-step behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real orchestrated chain end-to-end with grounding produced in evaluate stage (include a distinctive grounding fact). Assert final `plan` artifact includes/uses that grounding fact and that artifact lineage shows grounding persisted across stages.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback orchestration plus artifact-state integrity across components. MOCK-MISS: unit test of fallback helper alone may miss accidental overwrite/corruption in repository interactions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text, so not System. LLM-DEP: requirement is safe fallback and artifact integrity; can be verified with deterministic stub output, so not Agentic. MULTI-STEP: single-stage fallback behavior, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected higher levels because text is about absence-handling and storage integrity ('falls back safely ... without corrupting other stored artifacts'), which is local module wiring/state management.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage flow with no `grounding` artifact and with other unrelated artifacts present. Assert a schema-valid `plan` artifact is emitted, no exception is raised, and unrelated artifacts are unchanged byte-for-byte/metadata-for-metadata.
